nodes.name: Calculate ICP PersonScoring

parameters.prompt:
Please extract the following information from the LinkedIn profile page:

1. **Full Name**: Extract the full name of the individual.
2. **Current or Most Recent Job Title**: Identify the job title next to the logo of the current or last employer.
3a. **Current or Most Recent Employer**: Extract the name of the first company in the employment experience block. 
3b. Linkedin Company URL of the Current or Most Recent Employer: Extract the link of the first company in the employment experience block
4. **Location**: Extract the location of the individual.
5. **Number of Connections**: Extract the number of connections the individual has.
6. **Number of Followers**: Extract the number of followers the individual has.
7. **About Section Text**: Extract the text from the 'About' section.
8. **Interest Level in AI**: Determine the person's interest level in AI (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert).
9. **Seniority Level**: Determine the seniority level of the person (e.g., junior, mid-level, senior, executive).
10. **Technical Depth**: Determine the technical depth of the person (e.g., basic, intermediate, advanced, expert).
11. **ICP Score**: Calculate the ICP Score based on the following criteria:
    - AI Interest: beginner-5 pts, intermediate-10 pts, advanced-25 pts, expert-35 pts
    - Technical Depth: basic-5 pts, intermediate-15 pts, advanced-25 pts, expert-35 pts
    - Seniority Level: junior-5 pts, mid-level-15 pts, senior-25 pts, executive-30 pts
    - Sum the points to get the ICP Score.

Ensure that the extracted information is accurate and formatted according to the specified output schema.

For example, if the LinkedIn profile is of a senior software engineer with a strong interest in AI, return the following output:
{
  "full_name": "Jane Doe",
  "current_or_last_employer": "Tech Innovations Inc.",
  "current_or_last_title": "Senior Software Engineer",
  "location": "San Francisco, CA",
  "number_of_connections": 500,
  "number_of_followers": 300,
  "about_section_text": "Experienced software engineer with a passion for developing innovative programs that expedite the efficiency and effectiveness of organizational success.",
  "ai_interest_level": "advanced",
  "seniority_level": "senior",
  "technical_depth": "advanced",
  "icp_score": 85
}

Auto-Generate FAQ Answers in Vtiger CRM with DeepSeek LLM and LangChain

workflow_json.name: Vtiger CRM answer FAQ by DeepSeek LLM

parameters.query_field: select * from Faq where faqstatus='Draft' order by id desc limit 1;

parameters.text: =Output as plain text, {{ $json.result[0].question }}

parameters.content:
### 🧠 Auto-Answer FAQ Drafts with AI  
**(Vtiger CRM + DeepSeek + LangChain)**

This workflow runs **every 1 minute** to:
- 📥 Fetch the latest `Draft` FAQ from Vtiger CRM
- 🤖 Use **DeepSeek LLM** via **LangChain** to generate a plain-text answer
- 📤 Automatically update the FAQ with the answer and mark it as `Published`
---
> 💡 **Note:**  
> This workflow uses a custom **Vtiger CRM** node from the **Community Nodes** registry.  
> To install it in your self-hosted n8n:
> 1. Go to `Settings` → `Community Nodes`
> 2. Click **Install Node** then enter:  
> ```bash
> n8n-nodes-vtiger-crm
> ```
---
> ✅ Perfect for teams that want to auto-fill answers and speed up knowledge base publishing!

meta.name: Automated UGC Video Generator with Gemini Images and SORA 2

parameters.content:
# UGC Video Generator Workflow

Generates product videos using Gemini (image) + SORA 2 (video). Accepts webhook with product/video prompts and API keys, polls for completion, stores in Google Drive, and logs to Google Sheets.

**Required connectors:**
- Google Sheets OAuth
- Google Drive OAuth

**Required API keys** (passed via webhook):
- Gemini API key
- OpenAI API key

---
Created by [Daniel Shashko](https://www.linkedin.com/in/daniel-shashko/)
Receives POST request with prompts and API keys
Generates product image using Gemini AI from prompt
Extracts base64 image data from Gemini API response
Creates video from prompt using OpenAI SORA model
Checks SORA video generation status via API call
Determines if video rendering is finished or pending
Pauses 1 minute then checks video status again
Downloads completed video file from OpenAI API endpoint
Saves video to Google Drive as MP4 file
Logs video details: product, URL, status, timestamp

parameters.content:
## 1. ZeroBounce Validation
**Bulk validate** email addresses with ZeroBounce.

This uses the [ZeroBounce Bulk Email Validator API](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/email-validation-api-quickstart/v2-send-file) to validate a bulk file containing email address and optional IP addresses.

This returns the status of the email addresses along with other useful information about the address and domain.

In this example, the file is created by the "Bulk Validation Send File" node from the input data provided. The node can also be used with an existing file, or used to combine multiple items into a single file.

The wait node waits up to 5 minutes for the webhook from ZeroBounce. After 5 minutes it checks the file status and either continues, waits longer or exits with a failure.

## Input data
Here we are using [Sandbox](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/email-validation-api-quickstart/v2-sandbox-mode) email addresses which don't use any ZeroBounce credits.

These can be used for testing and proving your workflows before connecting production data.

In a real workflow, the input data will usually be provided by the trigger, e.g. new file added.

## Filtered outputs
These are the output branches which have been filtered by their validation status and score.

Further actions could be taken with these filtered results, e.g. followed up using [Email Finder](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/email-finder-api) or [Domain Search](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/domain-search-api) using our n8n node.

## 3. Split results by score
Here is your output, this refined list can now be used with a high confidence that it will reach your intended recipient.

## 2. ZeroBounce A.I. Scoring
**Bulk A.I. score** email addresses with ZeroBounce.

This uses the [ZeroBounce Bulk A.I. Scoring API](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/ai-scoring-api/ai-scoring-api__send-file) to score a a bulk file containing email addresses.

This returns the scores for the email addresses.

In this example, a single file is created by the "Bulk Scoring Send File" node from the multiple input items provided. The node can also be used with an existing file.

The wait node waits up to 5 minutes for the webhook from ZeroBounce. After 5 minutes it checks the file status and either continues, waits longer or exits with a failure.

The merge node is used to merge the scoring results with the validation results.

## ZeroBounce Email Validation and Scoring

This template uses the [ZeroBounce](https://www.zerobounce.net) node to validate and score email addresses. It is designed to first check if an email is deliverable and then uses A.I. Scoring to assess the sender's deliverability confidence.

This is an example template to demonstrate ways to use the ZeroBounce node and how to manipulate the data with core n8n nodes.

A ZeroBounce API key is needed to use this template. Generate one [here](https://www.zerobounce.net/members/API).




![ZeroBounce Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zerobounce/n8n-nodes-zerobounce/main/icons/zerobounce-logo.svg)

### 1. Email Validation
The **`Validate email`** node sends the email address to the ZeroBounce API to determine its status (e.g., **`'valid', 'invalid', 'spam trap'`**).
* **Function:** Checks the email address against ZeroBounce's Email Validation API


### 2. A.I. Scoring
For emails confirmed as valid, the workflow proceeds to the **`Score email`** node.
* **Function:** Applies ZeroBounce's proprietary A.I. model to assess the quality of the email, returning a numerical score (**0 to 10**).


### 3. Output
The workflow concludes with the **`Filter by score` Switch Node**, which uses the ZeroBounce score to categorize the email into three confidence tiers:
* **High Score:** Indicates high confidence in deliverability and is ready for immediate campaigns.
* **Medium Score:** Suggests moderate risk; these might be suitable for re-engagement or specialized campaigns.
* **Low Score:** Indicates high risk, even if the status was technically `valid`. These should be suppressed to protect sender reputation.

This uses the [ZeroBounce Bulk A.I. Scoring API](https://www.zerobounce.net/docs/ai-scoring-api/ai-scoring-api__send-file) to score a a bulk file containing email addresses.

This returns the scores for the email addresses.

In this example, a single file is created by the "Bulk Scoring Send File" node from the multiple input items provided. The node can also be used with an existing file.

The wait node waits up to 5 minutes for the webhook from ZeroBounce. After 5 minutes it checks the file status and either continues, waits longer or exits with a failure.

The merge node is used to merge the scoring results with the validation results.

## ZeroBounce Email Validation and Scoring

This template uses the [ZeroBounce](https://www.zerobounce.net) node to validate and score email addresses. It is designed to first check if an email is deliverable and then uses A.I. Scoring to assess the sender's deliverability confidence.

This is an example template to demonstrate ways to use the ZeroBounce node and how to manipulate the data with core n8n nodes.

A ZeroBounce API key is needed to use this template. Generate one [here](https://www.zerobounce.net/members/API).




![ZeroBounce Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zerobounce/n8n-nodes-zerobounce/main/icons/zerobounce-logo.svg)

### 1. Email Validation
The **`Validate email`** node sends the email address to the ZeroBounce API to determine its status (e.g., **`'valid', 'invalid', 'spam trap'`**).
* **Function:** Checks the email address against ZeroBounce's Email Validation API


### 2. A.I. Scoring
For emails confirmed as valid, the workflow proceeds to the **`Score email`** node.
* **Function:** Applies ZeroBounce's proprietary A.I. model to assess the quality of the email, returning a numerical score (**0 to 10**).


### 3. Output
The workflow concludes with the **`Filter by score` Switch Node**, which uses the ZeroBounce score to categorize the email into three confidence tiers:
* **High Score:** Indicates high confidence in deliverability and is ready for immediate campaigns.
* **Medium Score:** Suggests moderate risk; these might be suitable for re-engagement or specialized campaigns.
* **Low Score:** Indicates high risk, even if the status was technically `valid`. These should be suppressed to protect sender reputation.

Zendesk Pending Ticket Follow-up System with Gmail, Google Sheets & ClickUp

workflow_json.name: Automate follow-ups for pending tickets in Zendesk

parameters.content:
## 🎯 Workflow Overview

This workflow automates Zendesk support ticket management:
- Fetches pending tickets daily
- Logs them to Google Sheets for tracking
- Sends follow-up emails to customers
- Creates ClickUp tasks for team reminders

**Schedule**: Runs Monday-Friday at 8 PM

## 📊 Data Retrieval

Fetches all pending tickets from Zendesk using the API.

**Configuration Required**:
- Zendesk API credentials
- Filter: status = 'pending'
- Returns all matching tickets

## 🔍 Smart Filtering

Filters tickets based on status and age:
- Only processes 'pending' status tickets
- Helps reduce noise and focus on actionable items

**Logic**: status === 'pending'

## 🛠️ Data Processing

Formats and enriches ticket data:
- Standardizes field names
- Calculates ticket age and priority levels
- Adds computed fields for better analysis
- Prepares data for downstream nodes

## 📊 Data Logging

Logs formatted ticket data to Google Sheets:
- Maintains historical record
- Enables reporting and analytics
- Updates existing tickets or creates new entries
- Uses Ticket ID as matching column

## ✅ Task Management

Creates ClickUp tasks for ticket tracking:
- One task per pending ticket
- Due date based on ticket priority
- Tags from Zendesk are preserved
- Helps team track follow-ups

## 📧 Email Generation

Generates personalized follow-up emails:
- Groups tickets by customer email
- Creates professional HTML emails
- Includes ticket details and next steps
- Maintains brand consistency

## 📤 Email Delivery

Sends professional follow-up emails via Gmail:
- Uses HTML formatted emails
- Personalizes content per customer
- Includes all relevant ticket information
- Maintains professional branding

**Requires**: Gmail OAuth2 credentials

# 🔐 Bot Configuration Requirements

## 🤖 Telegram Bot Setup for Private Channels

To use this workflow with private channels, your bot must be properly configured:

### 1. **Bot as Channel Administrator**
- Go to your private channel
- Click "Manage Channel" > "Administrators"
- Add your bot as an administrator
- Grant these essential permissions:
  ✅ **Read messages** (required for getChatMember)
  ✅ **View channel members** (required for subscription checks)
  ✅ **Post messages** (optional, for sending content)

### 2. **Channel ID Format**
For private channels, use the full numeric ID:
- ❌ Public channel: `@channelname`
- ✅ Private channel: `-1001234567890`

How To get your private channel ID:
1. Forward a message from your channel to  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Or send your channel invite link to [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. The bot will return the full numeric ID

### 3. **Bot Token Security**
- Store your bot token securely in n8n credentials
- Never hardcode tokens in workflow nodes
- Use credential references like `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

### 4. **Permission Troubleshooting**
If subscription checks fail:
- Verify bot is still an admin
- Check that "Read messages" permission is enabled
- Confirm channel ID format is correct (-100 prefix)
- Test manually with:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"`

# 🎯 Telegram Lead Magnet - Subscription Gate with UpSell Branch

## 📋 Overview
This workflow implements a subscription gate for a Telegram lead magnet campaign. Users must subscribe to a Telegram channel before they can access the lead magnet (e.g., a free resource, discount code, or exclusive content).

## 🇺🇦 Українською
Цей воркфлоу реалізує шлюз підписки для кампанії лід-магніту в Telegram. Користувачі повинні підписатися на канал Telegram, перш ніж зможуть отримати доступ до лід-магніту (наприклад, безкоштовного ресурсу, коду знижки або ексклюзивного контенту).
ЗНИЖКИ, КУПОНУ, ТОЩО... Можна додатково запропонувати щось купити :)

## 🎥 YouTube Video Placement
[Watch Tutorial Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example) - ADD Link after Approved Workflow
## 📝 Workflow Logic
1. **Trigger** - Listens for Telegram messages and callback queries
2. **Subscription Check Request** - Determines if user wants to check subscription status
3. **User Data Extraction** - Extracts user ID and callback data from the message
4. **Callback Acknowledgment** - Acknowledges button clicks to provide responsive UX
5. **Telegram API Call** - Checks if user is subscribed to the specified channel
6. **Subscription Validation** - Validates if user has proper subscription status (member, admin, creator)
7. **Conditional Routing** - Routes user based on subscription status:
   - ✅ Subscribed - Sends lead magnet
   - ❌ Not Subscribed - Sends subscription instructions
8. **Upsell Path** - Optional upsell sequence triggered by /ok command

## 🛠️ Configuration Notes
- Replace `inputyourid` with your actual Telegram channel ID (without @)
- Replace bot token placeholders with your actual Telegram bot token
- Update the lead magnet delivery message with your actual file/resource links
- Customize the upsell/cross-sell content as needed

## 📈 Use Cases
- Lead generation for Telegram channels
- Content gating for exclusive resources
- Community building through subscription requirements
- Marketing funnel automation

# 🎬 YouTube Video

## 📺 Tutorial Video
[![Watch Tutorial](https://img.youtube.com/vi/example/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example)

## 📖 Video Description
This tutorial video walks through the setup and configuration of this Telegram subscription gate workflow. Learn how to:
- Configure your Telegram bot
- Set up the subscription check mechanism
- Customize messages for your audience
- Implement bilingual support
- Add upsell sequences

## 🇺🇦 Опис відео
Це навчальне відео проведе вас через налаштування та конфігурацію цього воркфлоу шлюзу підписки в Telegram. Дізнайтеся, як:
- Налаштувати свого Telegram-бота
- Налаштувати механізм перевірки підписки
- Налаштувати повідомлення для вашої аудиторії
- Реалізувати двомовну підтримку
- Додати послідовності допродажів

> ⚠️ **Important:** Replace the placeholder YouTube link and thumbnail with your actual tutorial video.

# 🧠 Node Logic Explanation

## 🔔 Telegram Message Trigger
Listens for incoming messages and callback queries from users interacting with the bot.

## ❓ Subscription Check Request
Determines if the user interaction is a request to check their subscription status.

## 🔧 User Data Extraction
Extracts essential user information (ID, callback data) needed for subscription verification.

## ✅ Callback Acknowledgment
Provides immediate feedback to users when they click buttons, improving UX.

## 🌐 Telegram API Call
Uses Telegram's getChatMember API to verify if a user is subscribed to the channel.

## ✅ Subscription Validation
Checks if the user has an acceptable subscription status (member, administrator, creator).

## 🔄 Conditional Routing
Routes the workflow based on subscription status:
- Subscribed users receive the lead magnet
- Non-subscribed users receive instructions

## 📈 Upsell Sequence
Optional marketing sequence triggered by the /ok command to engage users further.

## 🎯 Bilingual Support
All messages are provided in both Ukrainian and English for international reach.

# 🇺🇦 Налаштування бота для приватних каналів
## Для використання цього воркфлоу з приватними каналами, ваш бот повинен бути належним чином налаштований:

### 1. **Бот як адміністратор каналу**
- Перейдіть до свого приватного каналу
- Натисніть "Керувати каналом" > "Адміністратори"
- Додайте свого бота як адміністратора
- Надайте ці необхідні дозволи:
  ✅ **Читати повідомлення** (необхідно для getChatMember)
  ✅ **Переглядати учасників каналу** (необхідно для перевірок підписки)
  ✅ **Публікувати повідомлення** (необов'язково, для надсилання контенту)

### 2. **Формат ID каналу**
Для приватних каналів використовуйте повний числовий ID:
- ❌ Публічний канал: `@channelname`
- ✅ Приватний канал: `-1001234567890`

Як отримати ваш приватний ID каналу:
1. Перешліть пост або повідомлення з свого каналу у цього бота  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Або відправте ваш Інвайт-лінк [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. Бот відповість вам структурно по внутрішнім ID ваших спільнот і про ваш персональний акаунт з якого робився запит

### 3. **Безпека токена бота**
- Зберігайте токен бота в безпеці в облікових даних n8n
- Ніколи не заносьте токени ботів у ноди HTTP воркфлоу
- Використовуйте посилання на облікові дані, як `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

Наприклад: прокидати ключ ви можете через назву свого Підключення, яке ви створювали у меню n8n. У випадку назви Credentials "TG Marketing Bot", то назва буде
{{ $credentials.tgmarketingbot.token }}`

### 4. **Вирішення Проблем**
Як бот не може нормально перевірити підписку і показує помилку:
- Упевніться що ваш створений бот admin у каналі
- Перевірте дозволи "Read messages" має бути enabled
- Перевірте ID формат (-100 prefix) і не має зайвих пробілів
- Протестуйте у браузері, ось таким запитом на акаунт, який точно є підписником каналу:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"` - це для приватного

  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=@idnamechannel&user_id=123456"` - цей для публічного

- Використовуйте посилання на облікові дані, як `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

Наприклад: прокидати ключ ви можете через назву свого Підключення, яке ви створювали у меню n8n. У випадку назви Credentials "TG Marketing Bot", то назва буде
{{ $credentials.tgmarketingbot.token }}`

### 4. **Вирішення Проблем**
Як бот не може нормально перевірити підписку і показує помилку:
- Упевніться що ваш створений бот admin у каналі
- Перевірте дозволи "Read messages" має бути enabled
- Перевірте ID формат (-100 prefix) і не має зайвих пробілів
- Протестуйте у браузері, ось таким запитом на акаунт, який точно є підписником каналу:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"` - це для приватного

  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=@idnamechannel&user_id=123456"` - цей для публічного

# 🔐 Bot Configuration Requirements

## 🤖 Telegram Bot Setup for Private Channels

To use this workflow with private channels, your bot must be properly configured:

### 1. **Bot as Channel Administrator**
- Go to your private channel
- Click "Manage Channel" > "Administrators"
- Add your bot as an administrator
- Grant these essential permissions:
  ✅ **Read messages** (required for getChatMember)
  ✅ **View channel members** (required for subscription checks)
  ✅ **Post messages** (optional, for sending content)

### 2. **Channel ID Format**
For private channels, use the full numeric ID:
- ❌ Public channel: `@channelname`
- ✅ Private channel: `-1001234567890`

How To get your private channel ID:
1. Forward a message from your channel to  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Or send your channel invite link to [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. The bot will return the full numeric ID

### 3. **Bot Token Security**
- Store your bot token securely in n8n credentials
- Never hardcode tokens in workflow nodes
- Use credential references like `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

### 4. **Permission Troubleshooting**
If subscription checks fail:
- Verify bot is still an admin
- Check that "Read messages" permission is enabled
- Confirm channel ID format is correct (-100 prefix)
- Test manually with:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"`

# 🎯 Telegram Lead Magnet - Subscription Gate with UpSell Branch

## 📋 Overview
This workflow implements a subscription gate for a Telegram lead magnet campaign. Users must subscribe to a Telegram channel before they can access the lead magnet (e.g., a free resource, discount code, or exclusive content).

## 🇺🇦 Українською
Цей воркфлоу реалізує шлюз підписки для кампанії лід-магніту в Telegram. Користувачі повинні підписатися на канал Telegram, перш ніж зможуть отримати доступ до лід-магніту (наприклад, безкоштовного ресурсу, коду знижки або ексклюзивного контенту).
ЗНИЖКИ, КУПОНУ, ТОЩО... Можна додатково запропонувати щось купити :)

## 🎥 YouTube Video Placement
[Watch Tutorial Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example) - ADD Link after Approved Workflow
## 📝 Workflow Logic
1. **Trigger** - Listens for Telegram messages and callback queries
2. **Subscription Check Request** - Determines if user wants to check subscription status
3. **User Data Extraction** - Extracts user ID and callback data from the message
4. **Callback Acknowledgment** - Acknowledges button clicks to provide responsive UX
5. **Telegram API Call** - Checks if user is subscribed to the specified channel
6. **Subscription Validation** - Validates if user has proper subscription status (member, admin, creator)
7. **Conditional Routing** - Routes user based on subscription status:
   - ✅ Subscribed - Sends lead magnet
   - ❌ Not Subscribed - Sends subscription instructions
8. **Upsell Path** - Optional upsell sequence triggered by /ok command

## 🛠️ Configuration Notes
- Replace `inputyourid` with your actual Telegram channel ID (without @)
- Replace bot token placeholders with your actual Telegram bot token
- Update the lead magnet delivery message with your actual file/resource links
- Customize the upsell/cross-sell content as needed

## 📈 Use Cases
- Lead generation for Telegram channels
- Content gating for exclusive resources
- Community building through subscription requirements
- Marketing funnel automation

# 🎬 YouTube Video

## 📺 Tutorial Video
[![Watch Tutorial](https://img.youtube.com/vi/example/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example)

## 📖 Video Description
This tutorial video walks through the setup and configuration of this Telegram subscription gate workflow. Learn how to:
- Configure your Telegram bot
- Set up the subscription check mechanism
- Customize messages for your audience
- Implement bilingual support
- Add upsell sequences

## 🇺🇦 Опис відео
Це навчальне відео проведе вас через налаштування та конфігурацію цього воркфлоу шлюзу підписки в Telegram. Дізнайтеся, як:
- Налаштувати свого Telegram-бота
- Налаштувати механізм перевірки підписки
- Налаштувати повідомлення для вашої аудиторії
- Реалізувати двомовну підтримку
- Додати послідовності допродажів

> ⚠️ **Important:** Replace the placeholder YouTube link and thumbnail with your actual tutorial video.

# 🧠 Node Logic Explanation

## 🔔 Telegram Message Trigger
Listens for incoming messages and callback queries from users interacting with the bot.

## ❓ Subscription Check Request
Determines if the user interaction is a request to check their subscription status.

## 🔧 User Data Extraction
Extracts essential user information (ID, callback data) needed for subscription verification.

## ✅ Callback Acknowledgment
Provides immediate feedback to users when they click buttons, improving UX.

## 🌐 Telegram API Call
Uses Telegram's getChatMember API to verify if a user is subscribed to the channel.

## ✅ Subscription Validation
Checks if the user has an acceptable subscription status (member, administrator, creator).

## 🔄 Conditional Routing
Routes the workflow based on subscription status:
- Subscribed users receive the lead magnet
- Non-subscribed users receive instructions

## 📈 Upsell Sequence
Optional marketing sequence triggered by the /ok command to engage users further.

## 🎯 Bilingual Support
All messages are provided in both Ukrainian and English for international reach.

# 🇺🇦 Налаштування бота для приватних каналів
## Для використання цього воркфлоу з приватними каналами, ваш бот повинен бути належним чином налаштований:

### 1. **Бот як адміністратор каналу**
- Перейдіть до свого приватного каналу
- Натисніть "Керувати каналом" > "Адміністратори"
- Додайте свого бота як адміністратора
- Надайте ці необхідні дозволи:
  ✅ **Читати повідомлення** (необхідно для getChatMember)
  ✅ **Переглядати учасників каналу** (необхідно для перевірок підписки)
  ✅ **Публікувати повідомлення** (необов'язково, для надсилання контенту)

### 2. **Формат ID каналу**
Для приватних каналів використовуйте повний числовий ID:
- ❌ Публічний канал: `@channelname`
- ✅ Приватний канал: `-1001234567890`

Як отримати ваш приватний ID каналу:
1. Перешліть пост або повідомлення з свого каналу у цього бота  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Або відправте ваш Інвайт-лінк [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. Бот відповість вам структурно по внутрішнім ID ваших спільнот і про ваш персональний акаунт з якого робився запит

### 3. **Безпека токена бота**
- Зберігайте токен бота в безпеці в облікових даних n8n
- Ніколи не заносьте токени ботів у ноди HTTP воркфлоу
- Використовуйте посилання на облікові дані, як `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

Наприклад: прокидати ключ ви можете через назву свого Підключення, яке ви створювали у меню n8n. У випадку назви Credentials "TG Marketing Bot", то назва буде
{{ $credentials.tgmarketingbot.token }}`

### 4. **Вирішення Проблем**
Як бот не може нормально перевірити підписку і показує помилку:
- Упевніться що ваш створений бот admin у каналі
- Перевірте дозволи "Read messages" має бути enabled
- Перевірте ID формат (-100 prefix) і не має зайвих пробілів
- Протестуйте у браузері, ось таким запитом на акаунт, який точно є підписником каналу:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"` - це для приватного

  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=@idnamechannel&user_id=123456"` - цей для публічного

# 🔐 Bot Configuration Requirements

## 🤖 Telegram Bot Setup for Private Channels

To use this workflow with private channels, your bot must be properly configured:

### 1. **Bot as Channel Administrator**
- Go to your private channel
- Click "Manage Channel" > "Administrators"
- Add your bot as an administrator
- Grant these essential permissions:
  ✅ **Read messages** (required for getChatMember)
  ✅ **View channel members** (required for subscription checks)
  ✅ **Post messages** (optional, for sending content)

### 2. **Channel ID Format**
For private channels, use the full numeric ID:
- ❌ Public channel: `@channelname`
- ✅ Private channel: `-1001234567890`

How To get your private channel ID:
1. Forward a message from your channel to  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Or send your channel invite link to [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. The bot will return the full numeric ID

### 3. **Bot Token Security**
- Store your bot token securely in n8n credentials
- Never hardcode tokens in workflow nodes
- Use credential references like `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

### 4. **Permission Troubleshooting**
If subscription checks fail:
- Verify bot is still an admin
- Check that "Read messages" permission is enabled
- Confirm channel ID format is correct (-100 prefix)
- Test manually with:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"`

# 🎯 Telegram Lead Magnet - Subscription Gate with UpSell Branch

## 📋 Overview
This workflow implements a subscription gate for a Telegram lead magnet campaign. Users must subscribe to a Telegram channel before they can access the lead magnet (e.g., a free resource, discount code, or exclusive content).

## 🇺🇦 Українською
Цей воркфлоу реалізує шлюз підписки для кампанії лід-магніту в Telegram. Користувачі повинні підписатися на канал Telegram, перш ніж зможуть отримати доступ до лід-магніту (наприклад, безкоштовного ресурсу, коду знижки або ексклюзивного контенту).
ЗНИЖКИ, КУПОНУ, ТОЩО... Можна додатково запропонувати щось купити :)

## 🎥 YouTube Video Placement
[Watch Tutorial Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example) - ADD Link after Approved Workflow
## 📝 Workflow Logic
1. **Trigger** - Listens for Telegram messages and callback queries
2. **Subscription Check Request** - Determines if user wants to check subscription status
3. **User Data Extraction** - Extracts user ID and callback data from the message
4. **Callback Acknowledgment** - Acknowledges button clicks to provide responsive UX
5. **Telegram API Call** - Checks if user is subscribed to the specified channel
6. **Subscription Validation** - Validates if user has proper subscription status (member, admin, creator)
7. **Conditional Routing** - Routes user based on subscription status:
   - ✅ Subscribed - Sends lead magnet
   - ❌ Not Subscribed - Sends subscription instructions
8. **Upsell Path** - Optional upsell sequence triggered by /ok command

## 🛠️ Configuration Notes
- Replace `inputyourid` with your actual Telegram channel ID (without @)
- Replace bot token placeholders with your actual Telegram bot token
- Update the lead magnet delivery message with your actual file/resource links
- Customize the upsell/cross-sell content as needed

## 📈 Use Cases
- Lead generation for Telegram channels
- Content gating for exclusive resources
- Community building through subscription requirements
- Marketing funnel automation

# 🧠 Node Logic Explanation

# 🇺🇦 Налаштування бота для приватних каналів
## Для використання цього воркфлоу з приватними каналами, ваш бот повинен бути належним чином налаштований:

### 1. **Бот як адміністратор каналу**
- Перейдіть до свого приватного каналу
- Натисніть "Керувати каналом" > "Адміністратори"
- Додайте свого бота як адміністратора
- Надайте ці необхідні дозволи:
  ✅ **Читати повідомлення** (необхідно для getChatMember)
  ✅ **Переглядати учасників каналу** (необхідно для перевірок підписки)
  ✅ **Публікувати повідомлення** (необов'язково, для надсилання контенту)

### 2. **Формат ID каналу**
Для приватних каналів використовуйте повний числовий ID:
- ❌ Публічний канал: `@channelname`
- ✅ Приватний канал: `-1001234567890`

Як отримати ваш приватний ID каналу:
1. Перешліть пост або повідомлення з свого каналу у цього бота  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Або відправте ваш Інвайт-лінк [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. Бот відповість вам структурно по внутрішнім ID ваших спільнот і про ваш персональний акаунт з якого робився запит

### 3. **Безпека токена бота**
- Зберігайте токен бота в безпеці в облікових даних n8n
- Ніколи не заносьте токени ботів у ноди HTTP воркфлоу
- Використовуйте посилання на облікові дані, як `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

Наприклад: прокидати ключ ви можете через назву свого Підключення, яке ви створювали у меню n8n. У випадку назви Credentials "TG Marketing Bot", то назва буде
{{ $credentials.tgmarketingbot.token }}`

### 4. **Вирішення Проблем**
Як бот не може нормально перевірити підписку і показує помилку:
- Упевніться що ваш створений бот admin у каналі
- Перевірте дозволи "Read messages" має бути enabled
- Перевірте ID формат (-100 prefix) і не має зайвих пробілів
- Протестуйте у браузері, ось таким запитом на акаунт, який точно є підписником каналу:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"` - це для приватного

  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=@idnamechannel&user_id=123456"` - цей для публічного

# 🔐 Bot Configuration Requirements

## 🤖 Telegram Bot Setup for Private Channels

To use this workflow with private channels, your bot must be properly configured:

### 1. **Bot as Channel Administrator**
- Go to your private channel
- Click "Manage Channel" > "Administrators"
- Add your bot as an administrator
- Grant these essential permissions:
  ✅ **Read messages** (required for getChatMember)
  ✅ **View channel members** (required for subscription checks)
  ✅ **Post messages** (optional, for sending content)

### 2. **Channel ID Format**
For private channels, use the full numeric ID:
- ❌ Public channel: `@channelname`
- ✅ Private channel: `-1001234567890`

How To get your private channel ID:
1. Forward a message from your channel to  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Or send your channel invite link to [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. The bot will return the full numeric ID

### 3. **Bot Token Security**
- Store your bot token securely in n8n credentials
- Never hardcode tokens in workflow nodes
- Use credential references like `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

### 4. **Permission Troubleshooting**
If subscription checks fail:
- Verify bot is still an admin
- Check that "Read messages" permission is enabled
- Confirm channel ID format is correct (-100 prefix)
- Test manually with:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"`

parameters.content:
# 🔐 Bot Configuration Requirements

## 🤖 Telegram Bot Setup for Private Channels

To use this workflow with private channels, your bot must be properly configured:

### 1. **Bot as Channel Administrator**
- Go to your private channel
- Click "Manage Channel" > "Administrators"
- Add your bot as an administrator
- Grant these essential permissions:
  ✅ **Read messages** (required for getChatMember)
  ✅ **View channel members** (required for subscription checks)
  ✅ **Post messages** (optional, for sending content)

### 2. **Channel ID Format**
For private channels, use the full numeric ID:
- ❌ Public channel: `@channelname`
- ✅ Private channel: `-1001234567890`

How To get your private channel ID:
1. Forward a message from your channel to  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Or send your channel invite link to [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. The bot will return the full numeric ID

### 3. **Bot Token Security**
- Store your bot token securely in n8n credentials
- Never hardcode tokens in workflow nodes
- Use credential references like `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

### 4. **Permission Troubleshooting**
If subscription checks fail:
- Verify bot is still an admin
- Check that "Read messages" permission is enabled
- Confirm channel ID format is correct (-100 prefix)
- Test manually with:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"`

# 🎯 Telegram Lead Magnet - Subscription Gate with UpSell Branch

## 📋 Overview
This workflow implements a subscription gate for a Telegram lead magnet campaign. Users must subscribe to a Telegram channel before they can access the lead magnet (e.g., a free resource, discount code, or exclusive content).

## 🇺🇦 Українською
Цей воркфлоу реалізує шлюз підписки для кампанії лід-магніту в Telegram. Користувачі повинні підписатися на канал Telegram, перш ніж зможуть отримати доступ до лід-магніту (наприклад, безкоштовного ресурсу, коду знижки або ексклюзивного контенту).
ЗНИЖКИ, КУПОНУ, ТОЩО... Можна додатково запропонувати щось купити :)

## 🎥 YouTube Video Placement
[Watch Tutorial Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example) - ADD Link after Approved Workflow
## 📝 Workflow Logic
1. **Trigger** - Listens for Telegram messages and callback queries
2. **Subscription Check Request** - Determines if user wants to check subscription status
3. **User Data Extraction** - Extracts user ID and callback data from the message
4. **Callback Acknowledgment** - Acknowledges button clicks to provide responsive UX
5. **Telegram API Call** - Checks if user is subscribed to the specified channel
6. **Subscription Validation** - Validates if user has proper subscription status (member, admin, creator)
7. **Conditional Routing** - Routes user based on subscription status:
   - ✅ Subscribed - Sends lead magnet
   - ❌ Not Subscribed - Sends subscription instructions
8. **Upsell Path** - Optional upsell sequence triggered by /ok command

## 🛠️ Configuration Notes
- Replace `inputyourid` with your actual Telegram channel ID (without @)
- Replace bot token placeholders with your actual Telegram bot token
- Update the lead magnet delivery message with your actual file/resource links
- Customize the upsell/cross-sell content as needed

## 📈 Use Cases
- Lead generation for Telegram channels
- Content gating for exclusive resources
- Community building through subscription requirements
- Marketing funnel automation

# 🎬 YouTube Video

## 📺 Tutorial Video
[![Watch Tutorial](https://img.youtube.com/vi/example/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example)

## 📖 Video Description
This tutorial video walks through the setup and configuration of this Telegram subscription gate workflow. Learn how to:
- Configure your Telegram bot
- Set up the subscription check mechanism
- Customize messages for your audience
- Implement bilingual support
- Add upsell sequences

## 🇺🇦 Опис відео
Це навчальне відео проведе вас через налаштування та конфігурацію цього воркфлоу шлюзу підписки в Telegram. Дізнайтеся, як:
- Налаштувати свого Telegram-бота
- Налаштувати механізм перевірки підписки
- Налаштувати повідомлення для вашої аудиторії
- Реалізувати двомовну підтримку
- Додати послідовності допродажів

> ⚠️ **Important:** Replace the placeholder YouTube link and thumbnail with your actual tutorial video.

# 🧠 Node Logic Explanation

## 🔔 Telegram Message Trigger
Listens for incoming messages and callback queries from users interacting with the bot.

## ❓ Subscription Check Request
Determines if the user interaction is a request to check their subscription status.

## 🔧 User Data Extraction
Extracts essential user information (ID, callback data) needed for subscription verification.

## ✅ Callback Acknowledgment
Provides immediate feedback to users when they click buttons, improving UX.

## 🌐 Telegram API Call
Uses Telegram's getChatMember API to verify if a user is subscribed to the channel.

## ✅ Subscription Validation
Checks if the user has an acceptable subscription status (member, administrator, creator).

## 🔄 Conditional Routing
Routes the workflow based on subscription status:
- Subscribed users receive the lead magnet
- Non-subscribed users receive instructions

## 📈 Upsell Sequence
Optional marketing sequence triggered by the /ok command to engage users further.

## 🎯 Bilingual Support
All messages are provided in both Ukrainian and English for international reach.

# 🇺🇦 Налаштування бота для приватних каналів
## Для використання цього воркфлоу з приватними каналами, ваш бот повинен бути належним чином налаштований:

### 1. **Бот як адміністратор каналу**
- Перейдіть до свого приватного каналу
- Натисніть "Керувати каналом" > "Адміністратори"
- Додайте свого бота як адміністратора
- Надайте ці необхідні дозволи:
  ✅ **Читати повідомлення** (необхідно для getChatMember)
  ✅ **Переглядати учасників каналу** (необхідно для перевірок підписки)
  ✅ **Публікувати повідомлення** (необов'язково, для надсилання контенту)

### 2. **Формат ID каналу**
Для приватних каналів використовуйте повний числовий ID:
- ❌ Публічний канал: `@channelname`
- ✅ Приватний канал: `-1001234567890`

Як отримати ваш приватний ID каналу:
1. Перешліть пост або повідомлення з свого каналу у цього бота  [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
2. Або відправте ваш Інвайт-лінк [@getidsbot](https://t.me/getidsbot)
3. Бот відповість вам структурно по внутрішнім ID ваших спільнот і про ваш персональний акаунт з якого робився запит

### 3. **Безпека токена бота**
- Зберігайте токен бота в безпеці в облікових даних n8n
- Ніколи не заносьте токени ботів у ноди HTTP воркфлоу
- Використовуйте посилання на облікові дані, як `{{ $credentials.telegramApi.token }}`

Наприклад: прокидати ключ ви можете через назву свого Підключення, яке ви створювали у меню n8n. У випадку назви Credentials "TG Marketing Bot", то назва буде
{{ $credentials.tgmarketingbot.token }}`

### 4. **Вирішення Проблем**
Як бот не може нормально перевірити підписку і показує помилку:
- Упевніться що ваш створений бот admin у каналі
- Перевірте дозволи "Read messages" має бути enabled
- Перевірте ID формат (-100 prefix) і не має зайвих пробілів
- Протестуйте у браузері, ось таким запитом на акаунт, який точно є підписником каналу:
  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=-1001234567890&user_id=123456"` - це для приватного

  `curl "https://api.telegram.org/botYOURTOKEN@BOTFATHER/getChatMember?chat_id=@idnamechannel&user_id=123456"` - цей для публічного

parameters.content:
### 2. Use a LLM to keep only the procedures related to sustainability
We loop though all items parsed and we provide the description and the committee to a LLM (Open AI). The LLM will use these information to assess if the procedure is related to **sustainability** or not.

#### How to setup?

- **Open AI Node**:
   1. Add the required credentials Open AI credentials and select the model *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*
   2. Adapt the system prompt with the topic you want to filter out or keep.
  [Learn more about the AI Agent Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 3. Topics related to sustainability are stored in a Google Sheet
The output of the LLM is combined with the other fields. A IF node filters out all the procedure not related to sustainability. The remaining items are loaded in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?

- **Record outputs in the Google Sheet Node**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which you want to record your working sessions
   4. Map the fields: **Reference Number**. **Committee**, **Rapporteur**, **Title/Description**, **PDF Link**
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

parameters.content: ## Social Media Agent Tools
# 🏭Social Media Content Factory

## Create Post Image
https://pollinations.ai/
https://image.pollinations.ai/prompt/[your image description]

💡Notes

Update all Social Media Platform Credentials as required.

Adjust parameters and content for each platform to suit your needs.
# 👍 Approve Content Before Proceeding
## Prompt & Schema Composition from External Sources

parameters.text: ={{ $json.data.social_content.schema.post }}

parameters.content:
<system>
You are a specialized content creation AI for social media platforms.
Your primary function is generating platform-optimized social media content across various platforms including LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter (X), Threads, and YouTube Shorts. Each piece of content must:
Match the specific platform's audience expectations and algorithm preferences
Showcase relevant expertise in your field
Deliver actionable insights for your target audience
Drive meaningful engagement through value-driven content
OBJECTIVES:
Create platform-specific content following each platform's best practices
Implement strategic hashtag usage combining general and trending tags
Design content that encourages user interaction and community building
Maintain consistent brand voice while adapting to platform requirements
Incorporate data-driven insights to maximize content performance
OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
Deliver content in valid JSON format according to the platform-specific schema
Include all required fields as specified in the schema
Omit any explanatory text or code fencing in your response
Tailor content specifically to the platform indicated in the user's request
For each content request, adapt your output based on the platform guidelines.  Never provide URLS for video or image suggestions and only describe the suggestion.
</system>


<rules>
Only provide final response in valid JSON for the appropriate social platform
Never include any preamble or further explanation
Always remove any ``` ```json
</rules>


<linkedin>
**Style**: Professional and insightful.
**Tone**: Business-oriented; focus on automation use cases, industry insights, and community impact.
**Content Length**: 3-4 sentences; concise but detailed.
**Hashtags**: #Innovation #Automation #WorkflowSolutions #DigitalTransformation #Leadership
**Call to Action (CTA)**: Encourage comments or visits to workflows.diy's website for more insights.
</linkedin>

<instagram>
**Style**: Visual storytelling with creative captions.
**Tone**: Inspirational and engaging; use emojis for relatability.
**Content Length**: 2-3 sentences paired with eye-catching visuals (e.g., infographics or workflow demos).
**Visuals**: Showcase milestones (e.g., new workflow launches), tutorials, or product highlights.
**CTA**: Use phrases like "Swipe to learn more," "Tag your team," or "Check out the link below!"
**Link Placement**: Add the provided link before hashtags; if no link is provided, use "Visit our website: https://example.com"
**Hashtags**: #AutomationLife #TechInnovation #WorkflowTips #Programming #Engineering
</instagram>

<facebook>
**Style**: Friendly and community-focused.
**Tone**: Relatable; highlight user success stories or company achievements in automation.
**Content Length**: 2-3 sentences; conversational yet professional.
**Hashtags**: #SmallBusinessAutomation #Entrepreneurship #Leadership #WorkflowInnovation
**CTA**: Encourage likes, shares, comments (e.g., "What's your favorite automation tip?").
</facebook>

<xtwitter>
**Style**: Concise and impactful.
**Tone**: Crisp and engaging; spark curiosity in 150 characters or less.
**Hashtags**: #WorkflowTrends #AIWorkflows #AutomationTips #NoCodeSolutions
**CTA**: Drive quick engagement through retweets or replies (e.g., "What's your go-to n8n workflow?").
</xtwitter>

<threads>
**Style**: Conversational and community-driven posts.
**Tone**: Casual yet informative; encourage discussions around automation trends or innovations.
**Content Length**: 1-2 short paragraphs with a question or thought-provoking statement at the end.
**Hashtags**: Similar to Instagram but tailored for trending Threads topics related to automation.
</threads>

<youtube_short>
**Style**: Short-form video content showcasing quick workflow tutorials or use cases.
**Tone**: Authoritative yet approachable; establish workflows.diy as a leader in n8n automation solutions.
**Content Length**:
  Tutorials/Reviews (long-form): 5-10 minutes
  Shorts/Highlights (short-form): Under 1 minute
**CTA**: Encourage subscriptions, likes, comments (e.g., "Subscribe for more workflow tips!").
</youtube_short>

options.systemMessage:
## RULES
- You do not answer the users questions directly and your sole purpose is to call the appropriate tool to and provide the verbatim response.

## TOOLS
- create_x_twitter_posts_tool: Use this tool to create X-Twitter posts
- create_instagram_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Instagram posts
- create_facebook_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Facebook posts
- create_linkedin_posts_tool: Use this tool to create LinkedIn posts
- create_threads_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Threads posts
- create_youtube_short_tool: Use this tool to create a YouTube short

parameters.content: ## Prepare Email Approval Contents as HTML
# 💫Features & Benefits

## 1️⃣System Prompt Composition from External Source
Centralized prompt management: Store and update system prompts in Google Docs for easy team collaboration

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains consistent tone and style across platforms

- Flexible customization: Quickly modify prompts without changing workflow code

- Version control: Track changes to prompts over time with Google Docs revision history

- Role-specific access: Control who can edit core prompts while allowing broader viewing access

## 2️⃣Output Schema Composition from External Source
- Structured content generation: Enforce consistent JSON output formats for each platform

- Platform-specific optimization: Tailor content structure to each social network's requirements

- Reduced errors: Validate content against schemas before publishing

- Easy updates: Modify schemas as platform requirements change without workflow modifications

- Standardized metadata: Ensure all required fields are included for each platform

## 4️⃣ Dynamic Social Media Content Creator Agent with Web Search Tool to Match Social Post and Platform Schema

- Real-time research: Incorporate current events and trending topics into content

- Fact-checking: Verify information before publishing to maintain credibility

- Competitive analysis: Reference industry trends and competitor content

- Contextual relevance: Create content that responds to current market conditions

- Enhanced engagement: Generate content that aligns with trending conversations

## 5️⃣ Dynamic Image Creation to Match Social Post & Platform Schema

- Visual consistency: Generate platform-optimized images that match content themes

- Automated alt text: Create accessibility-compliant image descriptions

- Multi-format output: Generate images in various dimensions for different platforms

- Brand compliance: Ensure all visuals align with brand guidelines

- Reduced design bottlenecks: Eliminate waiting for custom graphics for each post

## 6️⃣ Image Archiving to Multiple Cloud Services for Future Use

- Redundant storage: Prevent content loss with multi-location backups

-Searchable repository: Build a library of past content for reference and reuse

- Asset tracking: Maintain records of which images were used for which campaigns

- Compliance documentation: Keep records of published content for regulatory purposes

- Resource optimization: Reuse successful visual assets for future campaigns

## 7️⃣ Telegram Messaging for Workflow Status 

- Real-time notifications: Get immediate alerts about workflow execution

- Error tracking: Quickly identify and address failures in the content pipeline

- Team coordination: Keep stakeholders informed of content progress

- Audit trail: Maintain records of when content was created and published

- Remote monitoring: Track workflow execution from mobile devices

## 8️⃣ Optional Workflow Reporting to Gmail in with Structured HTML Content
- Executive summaries: Provide management with clean, formatted reports

- Content approval: Enable stakeholders to review content before publishing

- Performance tracking: Document content metrics in standardized formats

- Schedule adherence: Monitor if content is being published according to plan

- Resource allocation: Track time and effort spent on different content types

## 9️⃣ Social Post Archiving to Google Drive for Future Use
- Content library: Build a searchable repository of all published content

- Performance correlation: Connect content with its performance metrics

- Compliance records: Maintain documentation of published materials

- Content repurposing: Easily find and adapt past successful content

- Campaign documentation: Group related content for campaign analysis

## 3️⃣Dynamic System Prompt and Platform Schema Composition based on User Prompt using External Sources
- Centralized prompt management: Store all system prompts in a single external source for easier maintenance and updates

- Platform-specific optimization: Automatically tailor content to each platform's unique requirements and audience expectations

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains your brand's tone and messaging guidelines across platforms

- Reduced technical debt: Modify prompts and schemas without changing workflow code or redeploying applications

- Collaborative improvement: Enable marketing teams to refine prompts without developer intervention

- Version control: Track changes to prompts and schemas over time with document revision history

- A/B testing capability: Easily test different prompt variations to optimize content performance

- Scalable content strategy: Add support for new platforms by simply creating new prompt and schema documents

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules

</xtwitter>

parameters.content: ## Prepare Email Approval Contents as HTML
# 💫Features & Benefits

## 1️⃣System Prompt Composition from External Source
Centralized prompt management: Store and update system prompts in Google Docs for easy team collaboration

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains consistent tone and style across platforms

- Flexible customization: Quickly modify prompts without changing workflow code

- Version control: Track changes to prompts over time with Google Docs revision history

- Role-specific access: Control who can edit core prompts while allowing broader viewing access

## 2️⃣Output Schema Composition from External Source
- Structured content generation: Enforce consistent JSON output formats for each platform

- Platform-specific optimization: Tailor content structure to each social network's requirements

- Reduced errors: Validate content against schemas before publishing

- Easy updates: Modify schemas as platform requirements change without workflow modifications

- Standardized metadata: Ensure all required fields are included for each platform

## 4️⃣ Dynamic Social Media Content Creator Agent with Web Search Tool to Match Social Post and Platform Schema

- Real-time research: Incorporate current events and trending topics into content

- Fact-checking: Verify information before publishing to maintain credibility

- Competitive analysis: Reference industry trends and competitor content

- Contextual relevance: Create content that responds to current market conditions

- Enhanced engagement: Generate content that aligns with trending conversations

## 5️⃣ Dynamic Image Creation to Match Social Post & Platform Schema

- Visual consistency: Generate platform-optimized images that match content themes

- Automated alt text: Create accessibility-compliant image descriptions

- Multi-format output: Generate images in various dimensions for different platforms

- Brand compliance: Ensure all visuals align with brand guidelines

- Reduced design bottlenecks: Eliminate waiting for custom graphics for each post

## 6️⃣ Image Archiving to Multiple Cloud Services for Future Use

- Redundant storage: Prevent content loss with multi-location backups

-Searchable repository: Build a library of past content for reference and reuse

- Asset tracking: Maintain records of which images were used for which campaigns

- Compliance documentation: Keep records of published content for regulatory purposes

- Resource optimization: Reuse successful visual assets for future campaigns

## 7️⃣ Telegram Messaging for Workflow Status 

- Real-time notifications: Get immediate alerts about workflow execution

- Error tracking: Quickly identify and address failures in the content pipeline

- Team coordination: Keep stakeholders informed of content progress

- Audit trail: Maintain records of when content was created and published

- Remote monitoring: Track workflow execution from mobile devices

## 8️⃣ Optional Workflow Reporting to Gmail in with Structured HTML Content
- Executive summaries: Provide management with clean, formatted reports

- Content approval: Enable stakeholders to review content before publishing

- Performance tracking: Document content metrics in standardized formats

- Schedule adherence: Monitor if content is being published according to plan

- Resource allocation: Track time and effort spent on different content types

## 9️⃣ Social Post Archiving to Google Drive for Future Use
- Content library: Build a searchable repository of all published content

- Performance correlation: Connect content with its performance metrics

- Compliance records: Maintain documentation of published materials

- Content repurposing: Easily find and adapt past successful content

- Campaign documentation: Group related content for campaign analysis

## 3️⃣Dynamic System Prompt and Platform Schema Composition based on User Prompt using External Sources
- Centralized prompt management: Store all system prompts in a single external source for easier maintenance and updates

- Platform-specific optimization: Automatically tailor content to each platform's unique requirements and audience expectations

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains your brand's tone and messaging guidelines across platforms

- Reduced technical debt: Modify prompts and schemas without changing workflow code or redeploying applications

- Collaborative improvement: Enable marketing teams to refine prompts without developer intervention

- Version control: Track changes to prompts and schemas over time with document revision history

- A/B testing capability: Easily test different prompt variations to optimize content performance

- Scalable content strategy: Add support for new platforms by simply creating new prompt and schema documents

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules

- You do not answer the users questions directly and your sole purpose is to call the appropriate tool to and provide the verbatim response.

## TOOLS
- create_x_twitter_posts_tool: Use this tool to create X-Twitter posts
- create_instagram_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Instagram posts
- create_facebook_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Facebook posts

parameters.content: ## Prepare Email Approval Contents as HTML
# 💫Features & Benefits

## 1️⃣System Prompt Composition from External Source
Centralized prompt management: Store and update system prompts in Google Docs for easy team collaboration

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains consistent tone and style across platforms

- Flexible customization: Quickly modify prompts without changing workflow code

- Version control: Track changes to prompts over time with Google Docs revision history

- Role-specific access: Control who can edit core prompts while allowing broader viewing access

## 2️⃣Output Schema Composition from External Source
- Structured content generation: Enforce consistent JSON output formats for each platform

- Platform-specific optimization: Tailor content structure to each social network's requirements

- Reduced errors: Validate content against schemas before publishing

- Easy updates: Modify schemas as platform requirements change without workflow modifications

- Standardized metadata: Ensure all required fields are included for each platform

## 4️⃣ Dynamic Social Media Content Creator Agent with Web Search Tool to Match Social Post and Platform Schema

- Real-time research: Incorporate current events and trending topics into content

- Fact-checking: Verify information before publishing to maintain credibility

- Competitive analysis: Reference industry trends and competitor content

- Contextual relevance: Create content that responds to current market conditions

- Enhanced engagement: Generate content that aligns with trending conversations

## 5️⃣ Dynamic Image Creation to Match Social Post & Platform Schema

- Visual consistency: Generate platform-optimized images that match content themes

- Automated alt text: Create accessibility-compliant image descriptions

- Multi-format output: Generate images in various dimensions for different platforms

- Brand compliance: Ensure all visuals align with brand guidelines

- Reduced design bottlenecks: Eliminate waiting for custom graphics for each post

## 6️⃣ Image Archiving to Multiple Cloud Services for Future Use

- Redundant storage: Prevent content loss with multi-location backups

-Searchable repository: Build a library of past content for reference and reuse

- Asset tracking: Maintain records of which images were used for which campaigns

- Compliance documentation: Keep records of published content for regulatory purposes

- Resource optimization: Reuse successful visual assets for future campaigns

## 7️⃣ Telegram Messaging for Workflow Status 

- Real-time notifications: Get immediate alerts about workflow execution

- Error tracking: Quickly identify and address failures in the content pipeline

- Team coordination: Keep stakeholders informed of content progress

- Audit trail: Maintain records of when content was created and published

- Remote monitoring: Track workflow execution from mobile devices

## 8️⃣ Optional Workflow Reporting to Gmail in with Structured HTML Content
- Executive summaries: Provide management with clean, formatted reports

- Content approval: Enable stakeholders to review content before publishing

- Performance tracking: Document content metrics in standardized formats

- Schedule adherence: Monitor if content is being published according to plan

- Resource allocation: Track time and effort spent on different content types

## 9️⃣ Social Post Archiving to Google Drive for Future Use
- Content library: Build a searchable repository of all published content

- Performance correlation: Connect content with its performance metrics

- Compliance records: Maintain documentation of published materials

- Content repurposing: Easily find and adapt past successful content

- Campaign documentation: Group related content for campaign analysis

## 3️⃣Dynamic System Prompt and Platform Schema Composition based on User Prompt using External Sources
- Centralized prompt management: Store all system prompts in a single external source for easier maintenance and updates

- Platform-specific optimization: Automatically tailor content to each platform's unique requirements and audience expectations

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains your brand's tone and messaging guidelines across platforms

- Reduced technical debt: Modify prompts and schemas without changing workflow code or redeploying applications

- Collaborative improvement: Enable marketing teams to refine prompts without developer intervention

- Version control: Track changes to prompts and schemas over time with document revision history

- A/B testing capability: Easily test different prompt variations to optimize content performance

- Scalable content strategy: Add support for new platforms by simply creating new prompt and schema documents

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules

json.content:
<system>
You are a specialized content creation AI for social media platforms.
Your primary function is generating platform-optimized social media content across various platforms including LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter (X), Threads, and YouTube Shorts. Each piece of content must:
Match the specific platform's audience expectations and algorithm preferences
Showcase relevant expertise in your field
Deliver actionable insights for your target audience
Drive meaningful engagement through value-driven content
OBJECTIVES:
Create platform-specific content following each platform's best practices
Implement strategic hashtag usage combining general and trending tags
Design content that encourages user interaction and community building
Maintain consistent brand voice while adapting to platform requirements
Incorporate data-driven insights to maximize content performance
OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
Deliver content in valid JSON format according to the platform-specific schema
Include all required fields as specified in the schema
Omit any explanatory text or code fencing in your response
Tailor content specifically to the platform indicated in the user's request
For each content request, adapt your output based on the platform guidelines and ensure it aligns with your organization's mission and values.  Never provide URLS for video or image suggestions and only describe the suggestion.
</system>


<rules>
- Only provide final response in valid JSON for the appropriate social platform
- Never include any preamble or further explanation
- Always remove any ``` ```json
</rules>

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules

parameters.content: ## Prepare Email Approval Contents as HTML
# 💫Features & Benefits

## 1️⃣System Prompt Composition from External Source
Centralized prompt management: Store and update system prompts in Google Docs for easy team collaboration

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains consistent tone and style across platforms

- Flexible customization: Quickly modify prompts without changing workflow code

- Version control: Track changes to prompts over time with Google Docs revision history

- Role-specific access: Control who can edit core prompts while allowing broader viewing access

## 2️⃣Output Schema Composition from External Source
- Structured content generation: Enforce consistent JSON output formats for each platform

- Platform-specific optimization: Tailor content structure to each social network's requirements

- Reduced errors: Validate content against schemas before publishing

- Easy updates: Modify schemas as platform requirements change without workflow modifications

- Standardized metadata: Ensure all required fields are included for each platform

## 4️⃣ Dynamic Social Media Content Creator Agent with Web Search Tool to Match Social Post and Platform Schema

- Real-time research: Incorporate current events and trending topics into content

- Fact-checking: Verify information before publishing to maintain credibility

- Competitive analysis: Reference industry trends and competitor content

- Contextual relevance: Create content that responds to current market conditions

- Enhanced engagement: Generate content that aligns with trending conversations

## 5️⃣ Dynamic Image Creation to Match Social Post & Platform Schema

- Visual consistency: Generate platform-optimized images that match content themes

- Automated alt text: Create accessibility-compliant image descriptions

- Multi-format output: Generate images in various dimensions for different platforms

- Brand compliance: Ensure all visuals align with brand guidelines

- Reduced design bottlenecks: Eliminate waiting for custom graphics for each post

## 6️⃣ Image Archiving to Multiple Cloud Services for Future Use

- Redundant storage: Prevent content loss with multi-location backups

-Searchable repository: Build a library of past content for reference and reuse

- Asset tracking: Maintain records of which images were used for which campaigns

- Compliance documentation: Keep records of published content for regulatory purposes

- Resource optimization: Reuse successful visual assets for future campaigns

## 7️⃣ Telegram Messaging for Workflow Status 

- Real-time notifications: Get immediate alerts about workflow execution

- Error tracking: Quickly identify and address failures in the content pipeline

- Team coordination: Keep stakeholders informed of content progress

- Audit trail: Maintain records of when content was created and published

- Remote monitoring: Track workflow execution from mobile devices

## 8️⃣ Optional Workflow Reporting to Gmail in with Structured HTML Content
- Executive summaries: Provide management with clean, formatted reports

- Content approval: Enable stakeholders to review content before publishing

- Performance tracking: Document content metrics in standardized formats

- Schedule adherence: Monitor if content is being published according to plan

- Resource allocation: Track time and effort spent on different content types

## 9️⃣ Social Post Archiving to Google Drive for Future Use
- Content library: Build a searchable repository of all published content

- Performance correlation: Connect content with its performance metrics

- Compliance records: Maintain documentation of published materials

- Content repurposing: Easily find and adapt past successful content

- Campaign documentation: Group related content for campaign analysis

## 3️⃣Dynamic System Prompt and Platform Schema Composition based on User Prompt using External Sources
- Centralized prompt management: Store all system prompts in a single external source for easier maintenance and updates

- Platform-specific optimization: Automatically tailor content to each platform's unique requirements and audience expectations

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains your brand's tone and messaging guidelines across platforms

- Reduced technical debt: Modify prompts and schemas without changing workflow code or redeploying applications

- Collaborative improvement: Enable marketing teams to refine prompts without developer intervention

- Version control: Track changes to prompts and schemas over time with document revision history

- A/B testing capability: Easily test different prompt variations to optimize content performance

- Scalable content strategy: Add support for new platforms by simply creating new prompt and schema documents

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules

options.systemMessage:
## RULES
- You do not answer the users questions directly and your sole purpose is to call the appropriate tool to and provide the verbatim response.

## TOOLS
- create_x_twitter_posts_tool: Use this tool to create X-Twitter posts
- create_instagram_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Instagram posts
- create_facebook_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Facebook posts
- create_linkedin_posts_tool: Use this tool to create LinkedIn posts
- create_threads_posts_tool: Use this tool to create Threads posts

parameters.content: ## Prepare Email Approval Contents as HTML
# 💫Features & Benefits

## 1️⃣System Prompt Composition from External Source
Centralized prompt management: Store and update system prompts in Google Docs for easy team collaboration

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains consistent tone and style across platforms

- Flexible customization: Quickly modify prompts without changing workflow code

- Version control: Track changes to prompts over time with Google Docs revision history

- Role-specific access: Control who can edit core prompts while allowing broader viewing access

## 2️⃣Output Schema Composition from External Source
- Structured content generation: Enforce consistent JSON output formats for each platform

- Platform-specific optimization: Tailor content structure to each social network's requirements

- Reduced errors: Validate content against schemas before publishing

- Easy updates: Modify schemas as platform requirements change without workflow modifications

- Standardized metadata: Ensure all required fields are included for each platform

## 4️⃣ Dynamic Social Media Content Creator Agent with Web Search Tool to Match Social Post and Platform Schema

- Real-time research: Incorporate current events and trending topics into content

- Fact-checking: Verify information before publishing to maintain credibility

- Competitive analysis: Reference industry trends and competitor content

- Contextual relevance: Create content that responds to current market conditions

- Enhanced engagement: Generate content that aligns with trending conversations

## 5️⃣ Dynamic Image Creation to Match Social Post & Platform Schema

- Visual consistency: Generate platform-optimized images that match content themes

- Automated alt text: Create accessibility-compliant image descriptions

- Multi-format output: Generate images in various dimensions for different platforms

- Brand compliance: Ensure all visuals align with brand guidelines

- Reduced design bottlenecks: Eliminate waiting for custom graphics for each post

## 6️⃣ Image Archiving to Multiple Cloud Services for Future Use

- Redundant storage: Prevent content loss with multi-location backups

-Searchable repository: Build a library of past content for reference and reuse

- Asset tracking: Maintain records of which images were used for which campaigns

- Compliance documentation: Keep records of published content for regulatory purposes

- Resource optimization: Reuse successful visual assets for future campaigns

## 7️⃣ Telegram Messaging for Workflow Status 

- Real-time notifications: Get immediate alerts about workflow execution

- Error tracking: Quickly identify and address failures in the content pipeline

- Team coordination: Keep stakeholders informed of content progress

- Audit trail: Maintain records of when content was created and published

- Remote monitoring: Track workflow execution from mobile devices

## 8️⃣ Optional Workflow Reporting to Gmail in with Structured HTML Content
- Executive summaries: Provide management with clean, formatted reports

- Content approval: Enable stakeholders to review content before publishing

- Performance tracking: Document content metrics in standardized formats

- Schedule adherence: Monitor if content is being published according to plan

- Resource allocation: Track time and effort spent on different content types

## 9️⃣ Social Post Archiving to Google Drive for Future Use
- Content library: Build a searchable repository of all published content

- Performance correlation: Connect content with its performance metrics

- Compliance records: Maintain documentation of published materials

- Content repurposing: Easily find and adapt past successful content

- Campaign documentation: Group related content for campaign analysis

## 3️⃣Dynamic System Prompt and Platform Schema Composition based on User Prompt using External Sources
- Centralized prompt management: Store all system prompts in a single external source for easier maintenance and updates

- Platform-specific optimization: Automatically tailor content to each platform's unique requirements and audience expectations

- Consistent brand voice: Ensure all generated content maintains your brand's tone and messaging guidelines across platforms

- Reduced technical debt: Modify prompts and schemas without changing workflow code or redeploying applications

- Collaborative improvement: Enable marketing teams to refine prompts without developer intervention

- Version control: Track changes to prompts and schemas over time with document revision history

- A/B testing capability: Easily test different prompt variations to optimize content performance

- Scalable content strategy: Add support for new platforms by simply creating new prompt and schema documents

- Dynamic adaptation: Respond to platform algorithm changes by quickly updating external prompt documents

- Knowledge preservation: Maintain institutional knowledge about effective platform-specific content strategies

- Reduced onboarding time: New team members can understand content requirements by reviewing documented prompts

- Compliance management: Ensure all generated content follows legal and brand guidelines by centralizing rules
